```{r}
options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))
install.packages(c("geodata", "sf", "ggplot2", "dplyr"))

library(geodata)
library(sf)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Download shapefile via geodata
india <- gadm(country = "IND", level = 2, path = tempdir())
gujarat <- india[india$NAME_1 == "Gujarat", ]
gujarat_sf <- st_as_sf(gujarat)

# Check exact district names
print(gujarat_sf$NAME_2)

# 2. Your data
df <- data.frame(
  district = c("Amreli","Anand","Bhavnagar","Banaskantha","Bharuch",
               "Gandhinagar","Ahmedabad","Dahod","Junagadh","Kheda",
               "Jamnagar","Kutch-Bhuj","Mehsana","Narmada","Navsari",
               "Godhra","Patan","Surat","Vadodara","Porbandar",
               "Rajkot","Sabarkantha","Surendranagar","Valsad"),
  value = c(2.869610055, 0.789011293, 0.200215773, 0.249798873,
            -9.795597338, 1.591040319, 0.006262659, -13.943726290,
            -7.137939671, 0.789011293, -13.772039677, 3.252076304,
            4.783940439, -9.795597338, 5.149083595, -13.943726290,
            0.249798873, 12.926220655, 0.148470679, -7.137939671,
            -12.078305469, NA, 5.149083595, 2.615756196)
)

# 3. Fix name mismatches to match GADM spelling — adjust after checking print() above
name_map <- c(
  "Kutch-Bhuj" = "Kachchh",
  "Godhra"     = "Panch Mahals"
  # add others as needed
)
df$district_matched <- ifelse(df$district %in% names(name_map),
                               name_map[df$district],
                               df$district)

# 4. Merge
gujarat_merged <- gujarat_sf %>%
  left_join(df, by = c("NAME_2" = "district_matched"))

# 5. Plot
ggplot(gujarat_merged) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = value), color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low  = "red",
    mid  = "lightyellow",
    high = "blue",
    midpoint  = 0,
    na.value  = "grey80",
    name = "Value"
  ) +
  labs(
    title   = "Gujarat Districts",
    caption = "Grey = missing data"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    axis.text  = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks = element_blank(),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )

ggsave("gujarat_map.png", dpi = 300, width = 10, height = 7)
```

```{r}
install.packages(c("leaflet", "rjson"))

library(leaflet)

# Download Gujarat GeoJSON directly from web (no shapefile needed)
gujarat_geojson <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/datameet/maps/master/Districts/gujarat.geojson"

df <- data.frame(
  district = c("Amreli","Anand","Bhavnagar","Banaskantha","Bharuch",
               "Gandhinagar","Ahmedabad","Dahod","Junagadh","Kheda",
               "Jamnagar","Kutch","Mehsana","Narmada","Navsari",
               "Panchmahal","Patan","Surat","Vadodara","Porbandar",
               "Rajkot","Sabarkantha","Surendranagar","Valsad"),
  value = c(2.869610055, 0.789011293, 0.200215773, 0.249798873,
            -9.795597338, 1.591040319, 0.006262659, -13.943726290,
            -7.137939671, 0.789011293, -13.772039677, 3.252076304,
            4.783940439, -9.795597338, 5.149083595, -13.943726290,
            0.249798873, 12.926220655, 0.148470679, -7.137939671,
            -12.078305469, NA, 5.149083595, 2.615756196)
)

library(leaflet)
library(geojsonio)  # install.packages("geojsonio")

guj <- geojsonio::geojson_read(gujarat_geojson, what = "sp")

# Merge data
guj@data <- merge(guj@data, df, by.x = "DISTRICT", by.y = "district", all.x = TRUE)

pal <- colorNumeric("RdYlBu", domain = df$value, na.color = "grey")

leaflet(guj) %>%
  addPolygons(
    fillColor = ~pal(value),
    fillOpacity = 0.8,
    color = "white",
    weight = 1,
    label = ~paste0(DISTRICT, ": ", round(value, 2))
  ) %>%
  addLegend(pal = pal, values = ~value, title = "Value")
```
